mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when industrial capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate regime produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth falls below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical relationship with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or positive payroll surprises consistently precede negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Gross domestic product growth exceeding the 2.5% annualized threshold confirms robust macroeconomic expansion, prompting immediate positive repricing of equity risk premiums and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 crosses above 2.5% annualized growth threshold
failure_condition: GDPC1 releases above the threshold are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the threshold effect disappears when GDP growth is driven solely by inventory accumulation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment signals a synchronized supply and labor market contraction, triggering recession risk pricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the signal becomes positive when the divergence occurs during post-recession recovery phases.
